title: Globally, the number of people living in slums increased by 25% in just four years
subtitle: Total number of people living in slums
source: Urban Indicators Database, UN Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat). Retrieved from World Development Indicators (EN.POP.SLUM.UR.ZS).
visdescription: This is a waffle chart. It shows the number of people living in slums in 2018 and 2022 where each square of the waffles represents 1 million people.
square_represents: Every square represents 1 million people living in slums

_meta.approvalNote: auto bulk waffle · real CSV bind · gallery complete (Tier B bulk)

scenes.text: In [emphasis: 2018], hundreds of millions of people lived in slum conditions.
By [emphasis: 2022], the number had risen sharply — about [emphasis: 25% more] people.
Side by side: the global increase in people living in slums over four years.
